title: IAH vs Hobby: Which Houston Airport Should You Fly Into?
summary: A complete guide to Houston's two airports — amenities, locations, and which one makes sense for your trip.
content:
Houston is served by two major airports: George Bush Intercontinental (IAH) and William P. Hobby (HOU). Choosing the right one can save you time, money, and stress.

George Bush Intercontinental (IAH) is Houston's primary international airport, located about 23 miles north of downtown. It has five terminals and serves most major international carriers. IAH is the better choice for international flights, long-haul domestic routes, and if you're traveling to or from the north side of Houston (The Woodlands, Spring, Conroe).

William P. Hobby (HOU) is smaller, closer to downtown (about 7 miles), and primarily serves domestic routes via Southwest Airlines and a few other carriers. It's much easier to navigate with shorter security lines and quicker baggage claim. Hobby is ideal for short-haul domestic flights and if you're staying in downtown, Midtown, or the Medical Center.

Our recommendation: For international travel, fly into IAH. For domestic trips, especially with Southwest, Hobby is hard to beat. Either way, AvaLimo covers both airports with the same premium service — flight tracking included.

title: 10 Reasons to Book a Chauffeur for Your Next Business Trip
summary: From productivity to professionalism, here's why executives choose chauffeured travel over rideshares.
content:
Business travel is about efficiency, professionalism, and making the right impression. Here are 10 reasons why a professional chauffeur beats rideshares and taxis every time.

  1. Work en route — A quiet, private cabin lets you take calls, answer emails, or prep for meetings.
  2. Zero waiting — Your chauffeur tracks your flight and is ready when you land.
  3. Consistent quality — Every vehicle is inspected and cleaned before each ride.
  4. Professional image — Arrive in a luxury vehicle — it speaks volumes to clients.
  5. No surge pricing — What you quote is what you pay, every time.
  6. Meet & greet — Your chauffeur welcomes you at arrivals with a name sign.
  7. 24/7 availability — Late flight? Early morning? We're always on.
  8. Local expertise — Our drivers know Houston traffic, shortcuts, and construction.
  9. Luggage assistance — No hauling your own bags — we handle everything.
  10. Peace of mind — Fully insured, licensed, and vetted chauffeurs.

title: How to Choose the Perfect Wedding Limo in Houston
summary: Bride, groom, wedding party — what vehicle fits your day? Our guide to wedding transportation.
content:
Your wedding day deserves a grand entrance and a memorable exit. Here's how to choose the right vehicle for your special day.

For the Couple: The Mercedes S-Class offers elegant, intimate luxury for just the two of you. The Cadillac Escalade provides more space for the bride's dress and a commanding presence.

For the Wedding Party: Our Mercedes Sprinter seats up to 14 guests, perfect for transporting the entire bridal party together. Everyone arrives on time and in style.

For Guests: Consider group transportation for out-of-town guests or shuttle service between ceremony and reception venues.

Booking Tips: Reserve 3-6 months in advance for peak wedding season (April-June, September-November). Schedule a vehicle walkthrough before your big day.

title: Best Concert Venues in Houston & How to Get There in Style
summary: From NRG to the House of Blues — your guide to Houston's top music venues.
content:
Houston's music scene is legendary. Here are the top venues and how to arrive like a VIP.

Top Venues:

  • NRG Stadium — Beyoncé, Taylor Swift, Rolling Stones. Parking is $40+ — skip it with AvaLimo.
  • Toyota Center — Rockets games and major concerts. Downtown traffic is brutal — let us handle it.
  • House of Blues — Intimate shows in the Theater District. No parking worries.
  • 713 Music Hall — Rising artists and cult favorites. We know the best drop-off spots.
  • White Oak Music Hall — Outdoor venue with great vibes. Easy pickup after the show.

Concert Night Package: Round-trip service with wait time included. Your chauffeur stays with the vehicle so you can enjoy the show without rushing.

title: How Early Should You Arrive at IAH? A Complete Airport Timeline
summary: Everything you need to know about timing your arrival at Houston's busiest airport.
content:
Timing your airport arrival can make or break your travel experience. Here's the complete guide for IAH.

Recommended Arrival Times:

  • Domestic flights: 2 hours before departure
  • International flights: 3 hours before departure
  • Peak times (holidays, summer): Add 30-60 minutes

Terminal Guide:

  • Terminal A: United domestic — typically fastest security
  • Terminal B: Southwest, Spirit — moderate wait times
  • Terminal C: United main hub — allow extra time
  • Terminal D: International arrivals — customs can take 30-90 min
  • Terminal E: Mixed international — varies by time of day

With AvaLimo: We track your flight and adjust pickup time automatically. No stress about traffic or timing — just show up and fly.

title: Mercedes S-Class vs Cadillac Escalade: Which AvaLimo Vehicle Is Right for You?
summary: A detailed comparison of our two most popular luxury vehicles.
content:
Both vehicles offer premium luxury, but they serve different needs. Here's how to choose.

Mercedes S-Class:

  • Capacity: Up to 3 passengers
  • Best for: Executive travel, couples, airport transfers
  • Luggage: 2-3 large suitcases
  • Vibe: Understated elegance, whisper-quiet ride
  • Features: Massaging seats, premium sound, ambient lighting

Cadillac Escalade:

  • Capacity: Up to 6 passengers
  • Best for: Families, small groups, extra luggage
  • Luggage: 4-6 large suitcases
  • Vibe: Commanding presence, spacious comfort
  • Features: Third-row seating, elevated view, cargo space

Price: Both vehicles are competitively priced — contact us for a quote based on your specific trip.

title: Packing for a Weekend in Houston: What to Bring & Where to Go
summary: The ultimate Houston weekend itinerary for visitors and first-timers.
content:
Houston is big, diverse, and full of surprises. Here's your complete weekend guide.

What to Pack:

  • Light, breathable clothing (it's hot and humid!)
  • Comfortable walking shoes
  • Light jacket for air-conditioned venues
  • Umbrella or rain jacket (afternoon storms are common)
  • Sunglasses and sunscreen

Must-Visit Spots:

  • Museum District: 19 museums within walking distance
  • Space Center Houston: NASA's official visitor center
  • Buffalo Bayou: Kayaking, trails, and skyline views
  • The Heights: Boutique shopping and brunch spots
  • Downtown: Theater District, restaurants, and nightlife

Getting Around: Houston is spread out — rideshares add up fast. Book AvaLimo for airport transfers and a day of sightseeing with a dedicated chauffeur.
